moves.startingMovesNote: You start with Consecrated Flame and Invoke the Sun God, plus 1 more of your choice.

backgrounds.label: Auspicious Birth
backgrounds.description:
You were born in Stonetop, and that birth was marked by the God of Light. You were born during an eclipse, perhaps, or under the light of a bright new star? Maybe you bear a sun-shaped birthmark?

Whatever the sign, your connection to Helior was clear early on. You've a place of honor in Stonetop, though it'd be a lie to say you don't make some uneasy.

When one of your moves has you mark a debility, you may mark this background's circle instead, to no ill effect. Clear it when you Make Camp or Convalesce.

backgrounds.label: Itinerant Mystic
backgrounds.description:
They think of you as a self-important kook who comes through now and again, speaking in riddles and playing tricks with the light. Sure, they know there's something holy about you, but it's not like you're a priest or anything. Priests talk sense.

When you go off a-wandering, hold 1 Enigma if you're gone for days, 2 if you're gone for weeks, or 3 if you're gone for months. At the very start of play, hold 3 Enigma. Spend Enigma 1-for-1 to: return from your wandering exactly when and where you are needed, fully Outfitted; Know Things as if you rolled a 10+; or have What You Need to produce an oddly specific yet mundane item of Value 1 or less.

backgrounds.label: Soul on Fire
backgrounds.description:
You once led a worldly life, full of fear and doubt, base pleasures and petty grudges. But something happened. Injury, illness, a brush with death. Or just a moment of such profound misery and self-loathing that you thought you could go no further.

There, in the dark, Helior's light shined upon you, igniting in your soul, lifting you and filling you with a profound sense of purpose.

When you Persuade a group by preaching charity, mercy, and hope and roll a 7+, aside from the usual effect, choose 1:

  • your name and your message spread
  • someone approaches you, now or later, eager to know more.

origin.region: Lygos or some other point south

options.label: Apiary
options.description: beeswax, candles (close, area, lasts ~1 hr), honey, ◇ bee smokers, ◇ hat & veils, etc.

options.label: Books & scrolls
options.description: ○○○○○ uses: a collection of religious texts and sacred histories. Mark a use to Know Things about Helior, the Sun-Worshippers, or their lore as if you rolled a 10+.

options.label: Chandlery
options.description: tallow, wax, wicks, dye, ◇ molds; candles, wick-holders, holy candles (close, area, ~1 hr), lanterns, etc.

options.label: Distillery
options.description: ○○ uses: skins of fine whisky (grants advantage to Persuade), copper tubes, malt, ◇◇ firkins, stills, barrels, etc.

options.label: Glassworks
options.description: sand, ash, ◇ crucibles, ◇ blowpipes; bottles, flasks, lenses, lantern glass, etc.

options.label: Holy relics
options.description: ○○○ uses: sacred objects of Helior's worship. Expend a use to add +1 to a roll involving the god's favor or power. Restore 1 use each season.

options.label: Luthier's tools
options.description: gut strings, pegs, wood, varnish, ◇ chisels, ◇ clamps; instruments, repairs, etc.

stonetop.statsNote: Assign these scores to your stats: +2, +1, +1, +0, +0, -1

list.title: The Worship of Helior Is…
list.description: You are the appointed servant of Helior the Daybringer, god of the sun and light, beacon of hope and mercy. (choose 1)
… most common in Lygos and the south.
… a new thing, still unheard of by many.
… an old thing, forgotten by most.

list.title: In Stonetop's Pavilion of the Gods, Helior's Shrine Has…

list.description: … the place of highest honor, even if Tor is more popular.
… been well-tended and given due respect.
… recently been restored/established, perhaps by you.
… lived long ago, a figure of legend.
… died facing a mighty sorcerer or demon.
… wrote many works of sublime beauty.
… faced one of the Things Below.
… through years of study and devotion.
… when your predecessor passed them on.
… suddenly, at a moment of great need.
… after a visitation from Helior or one of his servants.
… when you first laid eyes upon the _______.

options.description: To go without so that others are better off.
To inspire others in the face of adversity.
To bring relief or comfort, to give second chances.
To spread the glory and worship of Helior.
To refuse to suffer an injustice or a lesser evil.

system.description: Imagine yourself and your kin in a cave lit by a single torch, entranced by shadow puppet stories. Imagine realizing there is a greater truth, and stepping out of the cave into the true Light of day. Would you not bring that Light back into the darkness, to set your people free?
